## mise
- Installed via the official mise apt repo, not the Ubuntu default
- Must be installed before any mise-managed tools are set up

## jq
- JSON processor — useful in scripts for parsing API responses

## mc
- Midnight Commander — two-pane terminal file manager

## btop
- Resource monitor with a clean TUI

## ripgrep
- Fast recursive search; command is `rg`

## bat
- cat replacement with syntax highlighting; installed as `batcat` on Ubuntu

## git-delta
- Syntax-highlighted diffs for git; configure in ~/.gitconfig

## visidata
- Terminal spreadsheet and CSV/JSON explorer; command is `vd`

## openssh-server
- Required for the SSH-first workflow

## qemu-guest-agent
- Required when running as a QEMU/KVM virtual machine; safe to skip on bare metal

## glow
- Markdown renderer for the terminal

## fzf
- Fuzzy finder; required by navi

## navi
- Interactive cheatsheet tool — requires fzf

## zellij
- Terminal multiplexer — preferred over tmux

## lazygit
- Terminal UI for git

## atuin
- Shell history sync and search — replaces Ctrl+R

## micro
- Terminal text editor with familiar keybindings (Ctrl+S, Ctrl+C, etc.)

## yazi
- Terminal file manager with image previews

## zoxide
- Smarter cd — use `z` to jump to frequently visited directories

## pyenv
- Python version manager installed into ~/.pyenv
